description:
It was discovered that Qt did not correctly handle certain memory
operations. If a user or automated system were tricked into opening a
specially crafted file, an attacker could possibly use this issue to
cause a denial of service or execute arbitrary code.

full_text:
Ubuntu Security Notices

USN-7923-1

 

 

 

 

 

 
USN-7923-1: Qt vulnerability

 

 

 

 
Publication date

 

 

 
11 December 2025

 

 

 

 

 
Overview

 

 

 
Qt could be made to crash or run programs as your login if it
opened a specially crafted file.

 

 

 

 

 
Releases

 

 

 

 22.04 LTS

20.04 LTS

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Open side navigation
 

 

 
Packages

 

 qtbase-opensource-src - Qt 5 libraries
 

 

 
Details

 

 

It was discovered that Qt did not correctly handle certain memory
operations. If a user or automated system were tricked into opening a
specially crafted file, an attacker could possibly use this issue to
cause a denial of service or execute arbitrary code.

 

 

 

It was discovered that Qt did not correctly handle certain memory
operations. If a user or automated system were tricked into opening a
specially crafted file, an attacker could possibly use this issue to
cause a denial of service or execute arbitrary code.

 

 

 

 
Update instructions

 

 

 

 

In general, a standard system update will make all the necessary changes.

 Learn more about how to get the fixes.
 

 

 

 

 
The problem can be corrected by updating your system to the following package versions:

 

 

 

 

 Ubuntu Release
 Package Version
 

 22.04

LTS jammy

 libqt5core5a – 

5.15.3+dfsg-2ubuntu0.2+esm2

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 libqt5gui5 – 

5.15.3+dfsg-2ubuntu0.2+esm2

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 

 20.04

LTS focal

 libqt5core5a – 

5.12.8+dfsg-0ubuntu2.1+esm2

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 libqt5gui5 – 

5.12.8+dfsg-0ubuntu2.1+esm2

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 

Reduce your security exposure

 

 Ubuntu Pro provides ten-year security coverage to 25,000+ packages in Main and Universe repositories, and it is free for up to five machines.
 

 

 Get Ubuntu Pro
 

 

References

 

 

 CVE-2024-25580
